name: Incline Dumbbell Curl
resistance_profile: Reclined bench draws the arms behind the torso, stretching the biceps long head under load — a lengthened-biased curl.
rom_notes: Let the arms hang back into a full stretch at the bottom; supinate as you curl.
cues: Sit back on a ~45-60 degree incline
Let the arms hang behind the body at the bottom
Curl and supinate, keep the elbows still
execution_steps: Set an incline bench to about 45-60 degrees and sit back against it, a dumbbell in each hand.
Let your arms hang straight down and slightly behind your torso for a full stretch.
Curl the dumbbells up and supinate (turn palms up) as you lift, keeping the elbows still.
Squeeze at the top, then lower fully until the biceps are stretched at the bottom.
good_when: You want a curl that loads the biceps hard in the stretched position for growth.
You want to limit cheating by fixing the upper arms behind the body.
bad_when: No adjustable incline bench is available (use a standing or preacher curl).
The deep stretch aggravates your shoulder or elbow.
